sourceMovie.title: The Untouchables
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Untouchables works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Public Enemies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Untouchables clicked for you, Public Enemies (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Untouchables clicked for you, Road to Perdition (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Untouchables?
faq.answer: Try Public Enemies, Road to Perdition, Widows—each shares DNA with The Untouchables while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Untouchables?
faq.answer: The Untouchables (1987) is best described as Crime, History, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Untouchables worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
